class SendWebmentionJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def endpoint_from_body(html)
    doc = Nokogiri::HTML(html)

    if !doc.css('[rel~="webmention"]').css("[href]").empty?
      doc.css('[rel~="webmention"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
    elsif !doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org/"]').css("[href]").empty?
      doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org/"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
    elsif !doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org"]').css("[href]").empty?
      doc.css('[rel="http://webmention.org"]').css("[href]").attribute("href").value
    end
  end

  def endpoint_from_headers(header)
    return unless header

    if (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel="[^"]*\s?webmention\s?[^"]*"/))
      matches[1]
    elsif (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel=webmention/))
      matches[1]
    elsif (matches = header.match(/rel="[^"]*\s?webmention\s?[^"]*"; <([^>]+)>/))
      matches[1]
    elsif (matches = header.match(/rel=webmention; <([^>]+)>/))
      matches[1]
    elsif (matches = header.match(/<([^>]+)>; rel="http:\/\/webmention\.org\/?"/))
      matches[1]
    elsif (matches = header.match(/rel="http:\/\/webmention\.org\/?"; <([^>]+)>/))
      matches[1]
    end
  end

  # Some pages could return a relative link as their webmention endpoint.
  # We need to translate this relative link to an absolute one.
  def uri_to_absolute(uri, req_uri)
    abs_uri = URI.parse(uri)
    if abs_uri.host
      # Already absolute.
      uri
    else
      abs_uri.host = req_uri.host
      abs_uri.scheme = req_uri.scheme
      abs_uri.port = req_uri.port
      abs_uri
    end
  end

  def send_webmention(source, target, endpoint)
    sp = Sponge.new
    sp.timeout = 10
    # Don't check SSL certificate here for backward compatibility, security risk
    # is minimal.
    sp.ssl_verify = false
    sp.fetch(endpoint.to_s, :post, {
      "source" => URI.encode_www_form_component(source),
      "target" => URI.encode_www_form_component(target)
    }, nil, {}, 3)
  end

  def perform(story)
    # Could have been deleted between creation and now
    return if story.is_gone?
    # Need a URL to send the webmention to
    return if story.url.blank?
    # Don't try to send webmentions in dev
    return if Rails.env.development?

    sp = Sponge.new
    sp.timeout = 10
    begin
      response = sp.fetch(URI::RFC2396_PARSER.escape(story.url), :get, {}, nil, {
        "User-agent" => "#{Rails.application.domain} webmention endpoint lookup"
      }, 3)
    rescue BadIPsError, NoIPsError, DNSError, Errno::ECONNREFUSED, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError, TooManyRedirects, Zlib::DataError
      # other people's DNS/hosting issues (usually transient); just drop the webmention on the floor
      return
    end
    return unless response

    wm_endpoint_raw = endpoint_from_headers(response["link"]) ||
      endpoint_from_body(response.body.to_s)
    return unless wm_endpoint_raw

    wm_endpoint = uri_to_absolute(wm_endpoint_raw, URI.parse(story.url))
    send_webmention(Routes.story_short_id_url(story), story.url, wm_endpoint)
  end
end

require "commonmarker"

class Markdowner
  COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS = {
    extension: {
      tagfilter: true,
      autolink: true,
      strikethrough: true,
      header_ids: nil,
      shortcodes: nil
    },
    render: {
      escape: true,
      hardbreaks: false,
      escaped_char_spans: false
    }
  }
  USERNAME_MENTION = /\B([@~]#{User::VALID_USERNAME})\b/o

  # Rerender all markdown (#1627) cached in db columns, a pattern that predates Rails frament
  # caching and our adoption of the feature (6ce15c4f in 2025-10). As we build confidence in
  # fragment caching we should drop these columns.
  def self.rerender_db_markdown!
    Comment.all.find_each { |c| c.update_columns markeddown_comment: Markdowner.to_html(c.comment, as_of: c.created_at) }
    ModNote.all.find_each { |n| n.update_columns markeddown_note: Markdowner.to_html(n.note, as_of: n.created_at) }
    Story.where.not(description: "")
      .find_each { |s|
        s.update_columns markeddown_description: Markdowner.to_html(
          s.description,
          allow_images: s.can_have_images?,
          as_of: s.created_at
        )
      }
  end

  # allow_images: transform ![](example.com/img.jpg) to a hotlinked image; default is to print a link to the URL
  # as_of: what timestamp to add @mention links as of, because users rename; default is nil which is overridden
  #        to Time.current for newly created models passing their nil created_at

class Mod::StoriesController < Mod::ModController
  include StoryFinder

  before_action :find_story!
  before_action :show_title_h1

  def edit
    @title = "Edit Story"

    if @story.merged_into_story
      @story.merge_story_short_id = @story.merged_into_story.short_id
      User.update_counters @story.user_id, karma: (@story.votes.count * -2)
    end
  end

  def update
    @story.last_edited_at = Time.current
    @story.is_deleted = false
    @story.editor = @user
    update_story_attributes

    if @story.save
      redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
    else
      render action: "edit"
    end
  end

  def undelete
    @story.is_deleted = false
    @story.editor = @user
    update_story_attributes

    if @story.save
      Keystore.increment_value_for("user:#{@story.user.id}:stories_deleted", -1)
    end

    redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
  end

  def destroy
    update_story_attributes

    if @story.user_id != @user.id && @story.moderation_reason.blank?
      @story.errors.add(:moderation_reason, message: "is required")
      return render action: "edit"
    end

    @story.is_deleted = true
    @story.editor = @user

    if @story.save
      Keystore.increment_value_for("user:#{@story.user.id}:stories_deleted")
      Mastodon.delete_post(@story)
    end

    redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
  end

  private

  def story_params
    ps = params.require(:story).permit(
      :title, :url, :description, :moderation_reason,
      :merge_story_short_id, :is_unavailable, :user_is_author, :user_is_following,
      tags: []
    )
    ps[:tags] = Tag.where(tag: ps[:tags] || @story.tags.map(&:tag))
    ps
  end

  def update_story_attributes
    @story.tags_was = @story.tags.to_a
    @story.attributes = story_params
  end
end

def can_have_suggestions_from_user?(user)
    if !user || (user.id == user_id) || !user.can_offer_suggestions?
      return false
    end
    return false if is_moderated?

    tags.each { |t| return false if t.privileged? }
    true
  end

  # this has to happen just before save because it depends on user/editor
  def check_tags
    u = editor || user

    if u&.is_new? &&
        (unpermitted = tags.select { |t| t.permit_by_new_users == false }).any?
      tags_str = unpermitted.map(&:tag).to_sentence
      errors.add :base, "New users can't submit stories with the tag(s) #{tags_str}"
      self.new_user_acculturation_error = ModMail.create_inviter_discussion! user: u, message: <<~MESSAGE
        Hi,

        #{u.username}: New users can't submit stories with the tag(s) #{tags_str} because they're for meta discussion or prone to off-topic stories.
        If a tag is appropriate for the story, leaving the tag off to skirt this restriction can earn a ban.

        #{u.invited_by_user&.username}: This is a good opportunity for you introduce #{u.username} to what's [topical](https://lobste.rs/about#topicality) in the community.

        If you need, you can talk to the mods in this conversation, but we'll probably stay out of it unless asked.

        #{submission_markdown}
      MESSAGE
      return
    end

    if (privileged = tags.select { |t| !t.can_be_applied_by?(u) }).any?
      raise "#{u.username} does not have permission to use privileged tag(s): #{privileged.map(&:tag).join(" ")}"
    end

    if tags.reject { |t| t.is_media? }.empty?
      errors.add(:base, "Must have at least one non-media (PDF, video) " \
        "tag.  If no tags apply to your content, it probably doesn't " \
        "belong here.")
    end
  end

  def comments_anchor
    "comments_#{token}"
  end

  def description=(desc)
    self[:description] = desc.to_s.rstrip
    self.markeddown_description = generated_markeddown_description
  end

  def description_or_story_text(chars = 0)
    s = if description.present?
      markeddown_description.gsub(/<[^>]*>/, "")
    else
      story_text&.body
    end

    if chars > 0 && s.to_s.length > chars
      # remove last truncated word
      s = s.to_s[0, chars].gsub(/ [^ ]*\z/, "")
    end

    HtmlEncoder.decode(s.to_s)
  end

  def domain_search_url
    "/search?order=newest&q=domain:#{domain}"
  end

  def fix_bogus_chars
    # this is needlessly complicated to work around character encoding issues
    # that arise when doing just self.title.to_s.gsub(160.chr, "")
    self.title = title.to_s.chars.map { |chr|
      if chr.ord == 160
        " "
      else
        chr
      end
    }.join("")

    true
  end

  def generated_markeddown_description
    Markdowner.to_html(description, allow_images: can_have_images?, as_of: created_at)
  end

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Let me write up the full audit.

---

# Story Contract Audit — Lobsters

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** Lobsters is a Rails (MariaDB) link-aggregator/forum — users submit *stories* (links or text posts), comment on them, vote/flag, tag, hide/save, and moderators edit/merge/delete them. Standard Rails layout under `app/`: `models/`, `controllers/`, `views/`, `jobs/`, `mailboxes/` (inbound email → comments), `mailers/`, plus shared helpers in `extras/` (`Markdowner`, `Utils`) and `lib/`.

**The central submission model is `Story`** at `app/models/story.rb` (1386 lines — the largest model). Everything hangs off it. The shared pieces it is built on:
- `app/models/concerns/token.rb` — the one concern it `include`s (`Story` line 206), public id generation/validation.
- `app/models/short_id.rb` — 6-char public slug generator (used in `assign_initial_attributes`, `story.rb:544`).
- `extras/markdowner.rb` — renders description → `markeddown_description`.
- `extras/utils.rb` — `URL_RE` (validation) and `Utils.normalize` (dedupe/normalized_url).
- `app/models/link.rb`, `app/models/story_text.rb`, `app/models/story_image.rb` — the attached-data satellites the teardown rework will touch.

Map for the next agent: model + scopes `story.rb:1-204`; constants/validations/callbacks `story.rb:206-294`; instance behavior `story.rb:296-1386`. Dependents reachable via `belongs_to :story` (8 models) and `Story.` references across controllers/jobs/mailbox/search/stats.

## Step 2 — The Story contract and what it is built on

**Concern included:** `Token` — `story.rb:206`, defined `app/models/concerns/token.rb:1-19`. Sets `self.token` on `after_initialize`, validates presence/uniqueness, and makes `token` write-once (`token=` raises if already set). `Story#header_anchor` (`story.rb:724`) and `#comments_anchor` (`story.rb:645`) depend on `token`.

**Associations other records hang off Story** (`story.rb:4-54`), grouped by teardown disposition — this is the heart of what the rework changes:
- **`dependent: :destroy`** (will be deleted with the story): `taggings` (`:18`), `votes` (`:36`), `links` (`:45`). Comments in code note these *fail* under `:restrict_with_exception`.
- **`dependent: :nullify`**: `merged_stories` (`:13`, self-join via `merged_story_id`).
- **`dependent: :restrict_with_exception`** (block deletion if present): `comments` (`:32`), `suggested_taggings` (`:21`), `suggested_titles` (`:23`), `hidings`/HiddenStory (`:42`), `savings`/SavedStory (`:43`), `story_text` (`:44`, `has_one`, `foreign_key: :id`), `incoming_links` (`:48`), `moderation` (`:52`), `read_ribbons` (`:53`), `saved_stories` (`:54`).
- **`belongs_to` w/ counter_cache**: `domain` (`:5`), `origin` (`:6`), `merged_into_story` (`:7`, counter `:stories_count`).
- **`belongs_to :user`** (`:4`); **through assocs**: `tags` (`:35`), `voters` (`:39`), `suggested_tags` (`:22`).

**Callbacks (the runtime contract on save/create):** `before_validation :assign_initial_attributes` (`:259`); `before_save :log_moderation, :fix_bogus_chars` (`:260-261`); `after_create :mark_submitter, :record_initial_upvote` (`:262`); `after_save :bust_comment_redirect_cache, :recreate_links, :update_cached_columns, :update_story_text` (`:263`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** the satellite tear-down is split three ways (`:destroy` vs `:restrict_with_exception` vs `:nullify`), and lobsters does **soft-delete** (`is_deleted`/`is_gone?`, `story.rb:761`) rather than row deletion for normal flows — `destroy` controller actions set `is_deleted=true` and `save` (`stories_controller.rb:62-65`), they never call `.destroy`. The `after_save` chain re-derives `links`, cached columns, and `story_text` on **every** save (`:263`).

## Step 3 — How a story is created and ranked (in order)

1. **Controller entry** — `StoriesController#create` `stories_controller.rb:21-53`: builds `Story.new(user:)`, `update_story_attributes` (`:458`), validates, then `Story.transaction { @story.save … }`.
2. **URL handling** — `Story#url=` `story.rb:1168-1204`: strips tracking params (utm/sk/gclid…), tattles on lobsters self-attribution (`ModNote.tattle_on_traffic_attribution!`, `:1181`), normalizes port, then sets `normalized_url = Utils.normalize` (`:1202`, `extras/utils.rb:10`) and `set_domain_and_origin` (`:1157` → builds `Domain`/`Origin`, bumps counter_caches).
3. **Validation layer** — `assign_initial_attributes` (`:543`, sets `short_id` via `ShortId.new(...).generate` `short_id.rb:11`, `score`, `hotness`, `last_edited_at`); validations `:211-227`; the big `validate do…end` `:265-294` (dup/banned/brigading checks, `check_tags` `:612`).
4. **Markdown rendering** — `description=` `story.rb:649-652` calls `generated_markeddown_description` (`:687`) → `Markdowner.to_html(description, allow_images:, as_of:)` (`extras/markdowner.rb`), stored in `markeddown_description`.
5. **after_create** — `mark_submitter` (`:846`, Keystore counter), `record_initial_upvote` (`:867`).
6. **after_save chain** (`:263`): `bust_comment_redirect_cache` (`:320`) → `recreate_links` (`:1132` → `Link.recreate_from_story!` `link.rb:66`, parses `parsed_links` `story.rb:1107`) → `update_cached_columns` (`:1136`) → `update_story_text` (`:1143`).
7. **Score/ranking machinery** — `calculated_hotness` `story.rb:550-580` (tag hotness_mod + author bump + merged comment/story points + time decay over `HOTNESS_WINDOW`); persisted by `update_cached_columns` (`:1140`) and recomputed transactionally by `update_score_and_recalculate!` (`:693-718`, raw SQL recompute of `score`/`flags`/`hotness`). Bulk: `recalculate_all_hotnesses!` (`:488`).
8. **Post-commit jobs** — `stories_controller.rb:39-42`: `SendWebmentionJob` (5-min delay) and `CreateStoryCardJob` (story image).

## Step 4 — Every dependent of the Story contract

### Content & rendering
1. `app/models/link.rb:4` — `belongs_to :from_story`; `:66 recreate_from_story!` delete_all+rebuild on save.
2. `app/models/link.rb:6` — `belongs_to :to_story`; on-site URL → links story (`url=` `:43-49`).
3. `app/models/story_text.rb:6` — `belongs_to :story, foreign_key: :id`; full-text body cache, `fill_cache!` `:16` via DiffBot.
4. `app/models/story_image.rb:11-12` — wraps a Story/short_id to render OG card image.
5. `extras/markdowner.rb:30-35` — `rerender_db_markdown!` rewrites every story's `markeddown_description`.
6. `app/jobs/create_story_card_job.rb:5` — `StoryImage.new(story).generate(story.url)`.
7. `app/jobs/send_webmention_job.rb` (perform) — guards on `story.is_gone?`/`url`, sends webmention for the story URL.
8. `app/models/story.rb:1107 parsed_links` / `:1132 recreate_links` — derives `Link`s from url+markeddown_description.
9. `app/controllers/comments_controller.rb:96-103 redirect_from_short_id` — `c_<id>` cache mapping comment→story title path (busted by `story.rb:320`).

## Step 5 — How story identity & visibility are guarded

The contract is "real (persisted), current (not merged/deleted), visible (not hidden/gone, permitted to viewer)." Guards by area:

- **Identity / lookup:** `StoryFinder#find_story` `story_finder.rb:4-18` resolves by `short_id` (PK only for admins), `find_story!` raises `RecordNotFound` (`:20-25`). `Story.//short_id` `story.rb:296` (`find_by!`). `find_user_story` `stories_controller.rb:475-488` scopes to owner unless moderator.
- **Existence/current in scopes:** `base` `story.rb:61-65` chains `not_deleted` (`:76`) + `unmerged` (`:79`, default). `merged_into_story` redirect checks: `show` `stories_controller.rb:142`, `upvote` `:259`, `hide` `:294`, `save` `:357`, `comment` redirect `comments_controller.rb:97`.
- **Visibility:** `is_gone?` `story.rb:761` (deleted OR banned-author+negative); `can_be_seen_by_user?` `:582` (gates `show`, `stories_controller.rb:166`); `not_hidden_by`/`hidden_by` `:148-159`; `positive_ranked` `:80` excludes negative from feeds.
- **Search indexing:** `Story.base(@searcher)` `search.rb:230` (applies not_deleted/unmerged/mod-preload); comment search uses `joins(:story)`/`story_text` — note `:140-165` join story but rely on `Comment.accessible_to_user` (`:118`) for visibility, not `Story.base`.
- **Stats:** `stats.rb:22,35` `Story.pluck` with **no** deleted/merged guard (intentional — counts all submissions historically). Worth flagging for the rework.
- **Inbound email:** `inbox_mailbox.rb:44-46` finds story by short_id; `required_info` `:71-75` `bounced!` when `parent.nil?` — but **no** `is_gone?`/merged check, so a reply to a deleted/merged story still creates a comment via `process` `:12`.
- **Moderation:** `mod/stories_controller.rb` uses `find_story!` (raises if missing); `Moderation.for_story` `:34`. `log_moderation` `story.rb:796` guards `new_record?`/editor identity before recording.

# Story Contract Audit — Lobsters

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** Lobsters is a Rails (MariaDB) link-aggregator/forum — users submit *stories* (links or text posts), comment on them, vote/flag, tag, hide/save, and moderators edit/merge/delete them. Standard Rails layout under `app/`: `models/`, `controllers/`, `views/`, `jobs/`, `mailboxes/` (inbound email → comments), `mailers/`, plus shared helpers in `extras/` (`Markdowner`, `Utils`) and `lib/`.

**The central submission model is `Story`** at `app/models/story.rb` (1386 lines — the largest model). Everything hangs off it. The shared pieces it is built on:
- `app/models/concerns/token.rb` — the one concern it `include`s (`Story` line 206), public id generation/validation.
- `app/models/short_id.rb` — 6-char public slug generator (used in `assign_initial_attributes`, `story.rb:544`).
- `extras/markdowner.rb` — renders description → `markeddown_description`.
- `extras/utils.rb` — `URL_RE` (validation) and `Utils.normalize` (dedupe/normalized_url).
- `app/models/link.rb`, `app/models/story_text.rb`, `app/models/story_image.rb` — the attached-data satellites the teardown rework will touch.

Map for the next agent: model + scopes `story.rb:1-204`; constants/validations/callbacks `story.rb:206-294`; instance behavior `story.rb:296-1386`. Dependents reachable via `belongs_to :story` (8 models) and `Story.` references across controllers/jobs/mailbox/search/stats.

## Step 2 — The Story contract and what it is built on

**Concern included:** `Token` — `story.rb:206`, defined `app/models/concerns/token.rb:1-19`. Sets `self.token` on `after_initialize`, validates presence/uniqueness, and makes `token` write-once (`token=` raises if already set). `Story#header_anchor` (`story.rb:724`) and `#comments_anchor` (`story.rb:645`) depend on `token`.

**Associations other records hang off Story** (`story.rb:4-54`), grouped by teardown disposition — this is the heart of what the rework changes:
- **`dependent: :destroy`** (will be deleted with the story): `taggings` (`:18`), `votes` (`:36`), `links` (`:45`). Comments in code note these *fail* under `:restrict_with_exception`.
- **`dependent: :nullify`**: `merged_stories` (`:13`, self-join via `merged_story_id`).
- **`dependent: :restrict_with_exception`** (block deletion if present): `comments` (`:32`), `suggested_taggings` (`:21`), `suggested_titles` (`:23`), `hidings`/HiddenStory (`:42`), `savings`/SavedStory (`:43`), `story_text` (`:44`, `has_one`, `foreign_key: :id`), `incoming_links` (`:48`), `moderation` (`:52`), `read_ribbons` (`:53`), `saved_stories` (`:54`).
- **`belongs_to` w/ counter_cache**: `domain` (`:5`), `origin` (`:6`), `merged_into_story` (`:7`, counter `:stories_count`).
- **`belongs_to :user`** (`:4`); **through assocs**: `tags` (`:35`), `voters` (`:39`), `suggested_tags` (`:22`).

**Callbacks (the runtime contract on save/create):** `before_validation :assign_initial_attributes` (`:259`); `before_save :log_moderation, :fix_bogus_chars` (`:260-261`); `after_create :mark_submitter, :record_initial_upvote` (`:262`); `after_save :bust_comment_redirect_cache, :recreate_links, :update_cached_columns, :update_story_text` (`:263`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** the satellite tear-down is split three ways (`:destroy` vs `:restrict_with_exception` vs `:nullify`), and lobsters does **soft-delete** (`is_deleted`/`is_gone?`, `story.rb:761`) rather than row deletion for normal flows — `destroy` controller actions set `is_deleted=true` and `save` (`stories_controller.rb:62-65`), they never call `.destroy`. The `after_save` chain re-derives `links`, cached columns, and `story_text` on **every** save (`:263`).

## Step 3 — How a story is created and ranked (in order)

1. **Controller entry** — `StoriesController#create` `stories_controller.rb:21-53`: builds `Story.new(user:)`, `update_story_attributes` (`:458`), validates, then `Story.transaction { @story.save … }`.
2. **URL handling** — `Story#url=` `story.rb:1168-1204`: strips tracking params (utm/sk/gclid…), tattles on lobsters self-attribution (`ModNote.tattle_on_traffic_attribution!`, `:1181`), normalizes port, then sets `normalized_url = Utils.normalize` (`:1202`, `extras/utils.rb:10`) and `set_domain_and_origin` (`:1157` → builds `Domain`/`Origin`, bumps counter_caches).
3. **Validation layer** — `assign_initial_attributes` (`:543`, sets `short_id` via `ShortId.new(...).generate` `short_id.rb:11`, `score`, `hotness`, `last_edited_at`); validations `:211-227`; the big `validate do…end` `:265-294` (dup/banned/brigading checks, `check_tags` `:612`).
4. **Markdown rendering** — `description=` `story.rb:649-652` calls `generated_markeddown_description` (`:687`) → `Markdowner.to_html(description, allow_images:, as_of:)` (`extras/markdowner.rb`), stored in `markeddown_description`.
5. **after_create** — `mark_submitter` (`:846`, Keystore counter), `record_initial_upvote` (`:867`).
6. **after_save chain** (`:263`): `bust_comment_redirect_cache` (`:320`) → `recreate_links` (`:1132` → `Link.recreate_from_story!` `link.rb:66`, parses `parsed_links` `story.rb:1107`) → `update_cached_columns` (`:1136`) → `update_story_text` (`:1143`).
7. **Score/ranking machinery** — `calculated_hotness` `story.rb:550-580` (tag hotness_mod + author bump + merged comment/story points + time decay over `HOTNESS_WINDOW`); persisted by `update_cached_columns` (`:1140`) and recomputed transactionally by `update_score_and_recalculate!` (`:693-718`, raw SQL recompute of `score`/`flags`/`hotness`). Bulk: `recalculate_all_hotnesses!` (`:488`).
8. **Post-commit jobs** — `stories_controller.rb:39-42`: `SendWebmentionJob` (5-min delay) and `CreateStoryCardJob` (story image).

## Step 5 — How story identity & visibility are guarded

The contract is "real (persisted), current (not merged/deleted), visible (not hidden/gone, permitted to viewer)." Guards by area:

- **Identity / lookup:** `StoryFinder#find_story` `story_finder.rb:4-18` resolves by `short_id` (PK only for admins), `find_story!` raises `RecordNotFound` (`:20-25`). `Story.//short_id` `story.rb:296` (`find_by!`). `find_user_story` `stories_controller.rb:475-488` scopes to owner unless moderator.
- **Existence/current in scopes:** `base` `story.rb:61-65` chains `not_deleted` (`:76`) + `unmerged` (`:79`, default). `merged_into_story` redirect checks: `show` `stories_controller.rb:142`, `upvote` `:259`, `hide` `:294`, `save` `:357`, `comment` redirect `comments_controller.rb:97`.
- **Visibility:** `is_gone?` `story.rb:761` (deleted OR banned-author+negative); `can_be_seen_by_user?` `:582` (gates `show`, `stories_controller.rb:166`); `not_hidden_by`/`hidden_by` `:148-159`; `positive_ranked` `:80` excludes negative from feeds.
- **Search indexing:** `Story.base(@searcher)` `search.rb:230` (applies not_deleted/unmerged/mod-preload); comment search uses `joins(:story)`/`story_text` — note `:140-165` join story but rely on `Comment.accessible_to_user` (`:118`) for visibility, not `Story.base`.
- **Stats:** `stats.rb:22,35` `Story.pluck` with **no** deleted/merged guard (intentional — counts all submissions historically). Worth flagging for the rework.
- **Inbound email:** `inbox_mailbox.rb:44-46` finds story by short_id; `required_info` `:71-75` `bounced!` when `parent.nil?` — but **no** `is_gone?`/merged check, so a reply to a deleted/merged story still creates a comment via `process` `:12`.
- **Moderation:** `mod/stories_controller.rb` uses `find_story!` (raises if missing); `Moderation.for_story` `:34`. `log_moderation` `story.rb:796` guards `new_record?`/editor identity before recording.
- **Jobs:** `SendWebmentionJob#perform` guards `story.is_gone?`+`url.blank?`; `CreateStoryCardJob` has **no** gone/merged guard (`create_story_card_job.rb:5`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

The change = how Story **stores and tears down attached data**. Risk ranking:

**Highest (will break / data-correctness):**
1. `Story` `dependent:` map `story.rb:13-54` — changing teardown disposition directly. `:restrict_with_exception` on `comments/story_text/moderation/hidings/savings/read_ribbons/incoming_links/suggested_*` will **raise** on `.destroy` if not migrated; `:destroy` on `votes/taggings/links` cascades.
2. `after_save` chain `story.rb:263` — `recreate_links` (`:1132`, delete_all+rebuild every save), `update_story_text` (`:1143`), `update_cached_columns` (`:1136`). Any teardown that changes url/description re-derives Links; a story_text contract change breaks `:1147`.
3. `Link.recreate_from_story!` `link.rb:66-71` + `incoming_links` (`story.rb:48`, `:restrict_with_exception`) — links *to* a torn-down story block deletion / dangle.
4. `Vote.update_score_and_recalculate!` path `vote.rb:181`, `story.rb:693` — score/flag/hotness recompute; `HiddenStory.hide_story_for_user` `hidden_story.rb:13` also calls it.
5. `StoryText` `foreign_key: :id` `story_text.rb:6` — shares Story's PK; teardown of story_text is coupled to the story row identity and full-text search (`search.rb:262,285`).

**High (likely break, less obvious):**
6. `merged_stories dependent: :nullify` + `calculated_hotness` merged-points math `story.rb:563` / `merged_comments` `:851` — merge/unmerge interacts with teardown and ranking.
7. `bust_comment_redirect_cache` `story.rb:320` + `comments_controller.rb:96` — stale `c_<id>` cache after merge teardown.
8. Counter caches `domain`/`origin`/`stories_count` `story.rb:5-9` — wrong counts if teardown bypasses callbacks (`update_column`/`delete_all` paths in `hidden_story.rb`, `inactive_user.rb:10`, `read_ribbon.rb`).
9. `inbox_mailbox.rb:44` — unguarded reply to torn-down story creates orphan-ish comment.

**Medium:**
10. Search `Story.base`/`story_text` joins `search.rb:230,262,285` — index correctness after story_text teardown.
11. `CreateStoryCardJob` `create_story_card_job.rb:5` — no gone/merged guard; image generation for torn-down story.
12. `Stats.pluck` `stats.rb:22,35` and `flagged_commenters.rb:66` — aggregate counts unaffected by guards.
13. `Moderation`/`mod_mail` story references `moderation.rb:15`, `mod_mail_reference.rb:6` — `restrict_with_exception` / polymorphic dangling.

**Must re-verify after change:** soft-delete (`destroy`→`is_deleted`) still works end-to-end; merge/unmerge; vote/flag/hide score recompute; link re-creation; story_text + search; counter caches; webmention/card jobs short-circuit on gone stories; email replies to dead stories bounce.
